source: The Riverside Park Hotel Inspection Report 24.03.2026.pdf
documentTitle: International Protection Procurement Services Inspection Report, in accordance with the terms and conditions of the contracted provision of International Protection Accommodation Services
issuingBody: Department of Justice, Home Affairs and Migration — International Protection and Integration | Civil Justice, 51 St Stephen's Green, Dublin 2, D02 HK52

centre.name: The Riverside Park Hotel
centre.address: Killarney Road, Codrum, Macroom, Co. Cork, F93 X859

areasInspectedChecklist: Cleaning arrangements for all areas in the centre to include waste disposal and pet management
Communal areas to include corridors, stairs, reception area, public bathrooms, laundry areas, recreation rooms, play areas and any other indoor facilities
External areas such as grounds, parking, access and any other outdoor facilities
General Security and Health & Safety Arrangements including notices and visiting arrangements
In house shop where appropriate including points, dates of food and range of food
Fire Safety, including registers, notices, records and maintenance of fire safety equipment
Medical equipment and notices such as first aid, etc.
Staff Training Records to include first aid, fire safety, DLP
Kitchen Area to include cooking stations, fridges/freezers, storage, snacks available, dining area, menus & food options as applicable
Bedrooms to include lighting, heating, doors, windows, bathrooms, notices, fire detection, towels, bedlinen, duvets, toiletries/sanitary products, general upkeep/cleanliness

registers.name: Full List of Staff Employed including roles & duties
registers.observations: 10 staff on site. 5 full time and 5 part time.

registers.name: Separate list of designated liaison persons for child protection
Appendix 2 visitor child-protection declaration record
registers.observations: All visitors verbally informed of child protection policies and asked to sign an Appendix 2 declaration.

registers.name: Visitor sign-in book
registers.observations: Located at reception area. Does NOT include a CSS declaration.

registers.name: Maintenance issues log
registers.observations: Residents report any issues to staff and it is logged in book. Any issues identified during inspections are also recorded. Maintenance issues actioned within a timely fashion (48-72hrs): Yes.

registers.name: Security roster
registers.observations: Security roster not available. Security provided by external company: Grennan Security. 24/7 PSA licenced security staff NOT in place.

registers.name: Fire Alarm (Panel) / Carbon Monoxide & Detection System Service Records
Fire Exit Doors/Means of Escape Inspections
Kitchen daily cleaning record
registers.observations: No record of daily cleaning of kitchen, food service & dining areas.

registers.name: Kitchen periodic deep-clean record
registers.observations: No record of periodic deep cleans of areas such as behind cookers/fridges etc.

issues: Knife, kettle and fridge in room
Fridge, fan, kettle and knife in room
Fridge, freezer, kettle and microwave in room
Floor appears to be rotted and should be repaired/replaced
Fridge, iron, fan, heater and food in room
Fridge, iron, kettle, iron in room
Fridge, iron, microwave, and knives in room
Fridge, microwave, and kettle in room

Fridge, kettle and alcohol in room
Family residing in room have left the centre

spaceStandard.note: Suitable occupancy = total room dimensions / 4.65. If a room has varying ceiling height, only the part 2.4m or higher counts.

findings.actionRequired: Fridges in the majority of rooms at the centre. Inspector informed residents that they needed to be removed as soon as possible. Confirmation that this has been done to be provided within 14 days of receipt of this report. Numerous other prohibited electrical items present in rooms. Management to carry out their own inspection and remove, and provide confirmation that this has occurred within 14 days of receipt of this report.

Mould and damp present in some rooms throughout the centre. Management to treat the issue where identified and provide photographic evidence that this has been resolved within 14 days of receipt of this report.
Several rooms are overcrowded in the centre but this may be managed locally by centre management through the reallocation of rooms.
Numerous prohibited electrical items in rooms. All items to be removed and confirmation provided within 14 days of receipt of this report.

Food in rooms throughout the centre. Residents to be reminded that food is only to be consumed in the dining area. Copies of daily cleaning records and deep cleaning records for kitchen areas to be submitted within 14 days of receipt of this report. Bug zapper in kitchen area to be repaired/replaced and photographic evidence to be submitted within 14 days of receipt of this report.
Knives present in several rooms. Management to remind residents that this constitutes a safety issue and all knives should be stored in the storage provided.

ragLegend.RED: Contractual breach/high risk: immediate corrective action
ragLegend.AMBER: Medium risk/partial non-conformance; resolution within agreed 14-day timeframe
ragLegend.GREEN: Low operational concern, monitor for improvement

sections.title: 1.1 Office Admin
keyPoints: Latest resident register available: Yes
Full list of staff employed including roles & duties: Yes — 10 staff on site, 5 full time and 5 part time
Separate list of designated liaison persons for child protection: Yes
Residents provided with house rules on arrival: Yes — explained at induction meeting with new arrivals by the reception/integration officer
Procedure to report maintenance issues: Yes — residents report issues to staff, logged in book; issues identified during inspections also recorded
Maintenance issues actioned within a timely fashion (48-72hrs): Yes
Self-catering points allocation: points issued to residents on a weekly basis via Soldo card
Grocery points allocated in line with IPAS guidelines: Yes
Bedrooms inspected by staff: one corridor per week, with 124 hours notice given to residents (as printed — likely 24 hours)
Residents are responsible for cleaning their own rooms
If rooms not cleaned sufficiently: staff will speak to residents and a warning will be issued if required

sections.title: 1.2 Child Protection

CSS completed using a risk assessment for the centre: Yes
CSS publicly displayed: Yes
All visitors verbally informed of child protection policies and asked to sign an Appendix 2 declaration: Yes
Record of the Appendix 2 maintained: Yes
DLPs have received Tusla-approved Children First Training: Yes
Centre uses the Appendix 5 form to formalise child minding arrangements: Yes

keyPoints: WCs (ratio 1:8 required): all rooms ensuite

sections.title: 3. Bedrooms — inspector checklist
keyPoints: Checklist items: fire notices on display; fire blanket in shared cooking and laundry areas; smoke alarm and hazard detection; trip hazards; no unauthorised electrical items in bedrooms; windows; doors; bathrooms (mould/damp); no obstructions to fire exits; residents have no complaints or issues; own-door accommodation provisioning (delph, cutlery, pots, pans, kettle, cooking appliances/utensils); resident numbers match bed management form; individual rooms allocated for specific requirements (health, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity); all rooms lockable; bedrooms contain beds, wardrobe/chest of drawers and lockable units; central storage area for large items; no bunk beds unless requested or persons under 15 (unless specified in contract); pillows, duvets, blankets, bed sheets and towels (minimum 2 sets); room dimensions

caveats: Capacity columns on page 1 read 'Provider Contract Capacity: 198' and 'Confirmed Contract Capacity: 200' in extraction order; the two-row header ('Provider Contract Capacity' / 'Confirmed Contract Capacity') is split across lines in the PDF, so the two values could conceivably be swapped (i.e. contracted 200 / confirmed 198).
Contractor is recorded in the document as 'Next Week and Co. Limited'; briefing context expected 'Peppard Investments' — transcribed as printed.
Address Eircode transcribed as printed: 'F93 X859' (F93 is a Donegal routing key, unusual for a Macroom, Co. Cork address).
Bedroom inspection notice period printed as '124 hours notice' — likely a typo for 24 hours; transcribed as printed in section 1.1 key points but flagged here.
Room table: several rows omit length/width and give only total m2 (rooms 224, 222, 220, 218, and all 13.95 m2 and 18.60 m2 rooms); nulls used for missing cells.

Room table: rows with a single number after the room number (301, 303, 304, 305, 307, 308, 115, 117, 109) were interpreted as current occupancy with bed configuration missing, based on comparison with fully-populated rows (e.g. 306 '1D,3S | 5').
Rooms 209 and 212 show bed config and total dimensions (18.60) but no current occupancy or suitable occupancy value in the extractable text.
Room 302 row contains only dimensions (13.95) and suitable occupancy (3); bed config and occupancy absent.
Room 224 bed config printed as '3 S/1D' — normalised to '3S/1D'.
Room 206 issues list prints 'iron' twice ('Fridge, iron, kettle, iron in room') — transcribed as printed.
Summary Details: 'Fire Safety Issues' and 'Security & Staffing' rows have no RAG box visibly checked in the extracted text (RED/AMBER/GREEN all unchecked) — priority recorded as null.
Summary Details: 'Evidence of resolution attached' Yes/No boxes are unchecked for every finding — recorded as null.
Page 9 contains no extractable text (appears to be a blank or image-only page).
Transport timetable text printed as 'within 14 days o receipt' (sic) — transcribed with the obvious reading 'of receipt'.
Room count discrepancy: the room table lists 54 rooms (6 empty) while the master details state occupancy '177 across 50 Rooms' — both transcribed as printed.
